root_text.an6.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an6.1:2.1: “Chahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassa.
root_text.an6.1:2.3: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu cakkhunā rūpaṁ disvā neva sumano hoti na dummano, upekkhako viharati sato sampajāno.
root_text.an6.1:2.8: manasā dhammaṁ viññāya neva sumano hoti na dummano, upekkhako viharati sato sampajāno.
root_text.an6.1:2.9: Imehi kho, bhikkhave, chahi dhammehi samannāgato bhikkhu āhuneyyo hoti pāhuneyyo dakkhiṇeyyo añjalikaraṇīyo anuttaraṁ puññakkhettaṁ lokassā”ti.
root_text.an6.1:3.2: Attamanā te bhikkhū bhagavato bhāsitaṁ abhinandunti.

translation_text.an6.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an6.1:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants,
translation_text.an6.1:2.1: “Mendicants, a mendicant with six qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.
translation_text.an6.1:2.3: It’s a mendicant who, when they see a sight with their eyes, is neither happy nor sad. They remain equanimous, mindful and aware.
translation_text.an6.1:2.4: When they hear a sound with their ears …
translation_text.an6.1:2.5: When they smell an odor with their nose …
translation_text.an6.1:2.6: When they taste a flavor with their tongue …
translation_text.an6.1:2.7: When they feel a touch with their body …
translation_text.an6.1:2.8: When they know an idea with their mind, they’re neither happy nor sad. They remain equanimous, mindful and aware.
translation_text.an6.1:2.9: A mendicant with these six qualities is worthy of offerings dedicated to the gods, worthy of hospitality, worthy of a religious donation, worthy of veneration with joined palms, and is the supreme field of merit for the world.”
translation_text.an6.1:3.1: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.an6.1:3.2: Satisfied, the mendicants approved what the Buddha said.

reference_text.an6.1:1.1: bj21.2, csp1ed16.247, csp2ed16.247, dr22.306, ms16A6_2, msdiv1, ndp18.3, pts-vp-pli3.279, vri38.1
